page: Google Cloud Platform ct6e-standard-4t is an x86_64 architecture server configured with 180 dedicated vCPUs and 720 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance features four integrated Google V6 lite TPUs, also designated as ct6e accelerators, to support accelerated computing workloads.

This server type does not include local NVMe storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. Additionally, it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The lack of local storage represents a key tradeoff for workloads requiring high-speed local scratch space, shifting the dependency entirely to network storage performance.

Given the integration of four Google V6 lite TPUs alongside a high vCPU count, the ct6e-standard-4t is designed for machine learning workloads, including model training, fine-tuning, and large-scale inference. The qualitative cost efficiency of this hardware profile is centered on its specialized TPU resource density, offering a consolidated platform for parallelized tensor computations without the overhead of general-purpose GPU architectures.
description: Google Cloud Platform ct6e-standard-4t is an x86_64 architecture server equipped with 180 dedicated vCPUs, 720 GB of RAM, and four integrated Google V6 lite TPUs. With a memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per vCPU, this instance is designed for highly parallelized machine learning and accelerated computing tasks. It features no local storage, requiring network-attached storage solutions for data persistence, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing performance, while the absence of local storage represents a key architectural tradeoff. The hardware profile is optimized for tensor processing and large-scale compute workloads, making it highly suitable for machine learning inference, model training, and data pipeline acceleration where specialized TPU hardware can be fully leveraged.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform ct6e-standard-4t is an x86_64 server featuring 180 dedicated vCPUs, 720 GB of RAM, and four integrated Google V6 lite TPUs designed for accelerated machine learning workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform ct6e-standard-4t features 180 dedicated vCPUs, 720 GB RAM, and four Google V6 lite TPUs for accelerated machine learning.
tagline: An accelerated computing instance featuring one hundred and eighty dedicated vCPUs and four integrated TPUs for parallelized machine learning workloads.
bullet_points: 180 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs and 720 GB of system memory
Four integrated Google V6 lite TPUs for tensor acceleration
No local storage, requiring network-attached storage solutions
Zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses included
Optimized for machine learning training, fine-tuning, and inference workloads
